title: SPARC Bilateral Phrenic Neurophysiology Preparation with Afferent Stimulation
author: Kristi Streeter
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bgfzjtp6
materials: Isolate bilateral phrenic nerves using a dorsal approach:

steps.title: Isoflurane induction
steps.instruction: Isoflurane induction: 2.5-3% in 100% O2 in a closed chamber for 3-5 minutes

steps.title: Transfer to nose cone and maintain...
steps.instruction: Transfer to nose cone and maintain at 3% in 100% 02.

steps.title: Place intravenous line into tail vein...
steps.instruction: Place intravenous line into tail vein for delivery of drugs/anesthesia/fluids (flush with heparinized saline). Reduce isoflurane anesthesia to 1%. Begin urethane infusion (1.8g/kg in ddH2O at 6ml/hr) while slowly decreasing isoflurane.Confirm adequate plane of anesthesia via toe pinch and transfer to surgical station.

steps.title: Measure body temperature via rectal probe and maintain at 37
steps.instruction: Measure body temperature via rectal probe and maintain at 37.0 +/- 0.5 C via heated surgical table

steps.title: Perform tracheostomy and initiate mechanical ventilation...
steps.instruction: Perform tracheostomy and initiate mechanical ventilation with the following parameters: rate = 70 breaths/minute; tidal volume = (0.7 x body weight (in grams)) mL; inspired gases: 50% O2 + a small percentage of CO2 added to gas mixture reach a target end-tidal CO2 to be maintained at ~ 45mmHg throughout the surgical procedure + balance N2

steps.title: Perform bilateral vagotomy
steps.instruction: Perform bilateral vagotomy

steps.title: Place femoral arterial line to monitor...
steps.instruction: Place femoral arterial line to monitor arterial blood pressure and withdraw periodic blood samples (pre-fill the catheter with heparinized saline and ensure no bubbles are present in the line)

steps.title: make a 1/2 inch ventral incision...
steps.instruction: make a 1/2 inch ventral incision just distal to the groin on the inner thigh to expose the femoral artery, vein and nerve

steps.title: gently dissect the surrounding fascia to isolate the femoral artery
steps.instruction: gently dissect the surrounding fascia to isolate the femoral artery

steps.title: tie off the distal end of...
steps.instruction: tie off the distal end of the femoral artery and place a loose suture at the proximal end. Using an alligator clamp, pull the suture to temporarily prevent blood flow at the proximal end of the femoral artery

steps.title: make a small partial incision into...
steps.instruction: make a small partial incision into the wall of the femoral artery and insert the arterial catheter into the proximal portion of the artery, carefully loosening the proximal suture to enable advancement of the catheter into the vessle (~1 cm into the proximal portion of the vessel.

steps.title: tie the catheter into the vessel...
steps.instruction: tie the catheter into the vessel using the proximal suture and secure in place with additional sutures as needed

steps.title: ensure no kinks in the vessel...
steps.instruction: ensure no kinks in the vessel and open the stop cock at the end of the catheter to ensure unresisted flow into the catheter

steps.title: flush the catheter with heparinized saline...
steps.instruction: flush the catheter with heparinized saline and connect to the pressure transducer/amplifier to ensure adequate blood pressure readings

steps.title: Using a cautery, perform a ~...
steps.instruction: Using a cautery, perform a ~ two inch incision just lateral to midline and medial to shoulder blade.

steps.title: Using a cautery, dissect the dorsal...
steps.instruction: Using a cautery, dissect the dorsal musculature connecting the medial border of the scapulae to the posterior trunk on both sides.

steps.title: Reflect the medial border of the...
steps.instruction: Reflect the medial border of the scapulae and secure with an alligator clip.

steps.title: Dissect the deep thoracic wall muscles...
steps.instruction: Dissect the deep thoracic wall muscles and overlying fascia to expose the brachial plexus. Using suture, visulaize the tendon running horizontal above brachial plexus, pass 4.0 suture under tendon twice and place basket stich around tendon. Pull suture toward midline of animal and secure to open phrenic pocket.

steps.title: Cut the brachial plexus distally and...
steps.instruction: Cut the brachial plexus distally and reflect the nerve bundle to expose the phrenic nerve

steps.title: Using fine forceps, carefully isolate the...
steps.instruction: Using fine forceps, carefully isolate the phrenic nerve from the surrounding fascia without touching or pulling on the nerve

steps.title: Once the nerve is fully isolated,...
steps.instruction: Once the nerve is fully isolated, section the nerve distally and carefully desheath the proximal end of the nerve (~1 mm). Add 0.9% saline to phrenic pocket.

steps.title: Repeat to isolate right phrenic nerve
steps.instruction: Repeat to isolate right phrenic nerve.

steps.title: In groups recieving a dorsal rhizotomy
steps.instruction: In groups recieving a dorsal rhizotomy. Cut left C3, C4, C5, C6 dorsal rootlets

steps.title: Using cautery, make a ~1inch medial...
steps.instruction: Using cautery, make a ~1inch medial incision from base of skull.

steps.title: cut through underlying muscles to expose verterbral processes
steps.instruction: cut through underlying muscles to expose verterbral processes. Using cautery, seperate musculature from C2 vertebrae. Using microcurette remove muscles from C3-C6 vertebrae.

steps.title: Using ronguers, remove the left portion of C3-C6 vertebrae
steps.instruction: Using ronguers, remove the left portion of C3-C6 vertebrae.

steps.title: Using microscissors and fine forceps, cut...
steps.instruction: Using microscissors and fine forceps, cut and remove dura over C3-C6.

steps.title: visuallize and cut all dorsal rootlets from C3-C6 with microscissors
steps.instruction: visuallize and cut all dorsal rootlets from C3-C6 with microscissors.

steps.title: Add saline soaked cotton above spinal cord
steps.instruction: Add saline soaked cotton above spinal cord

steps.title: Set up phrenic nerve recordings
steps.instruction: Set up phrenic nerve recordings.

steps.title: Place magentic base with L-shaped rod...
steps.instruction: Place magentic base with L-shaped rod so that the rod is running lenth wise with the rat and above the midline.

steps.title: Pull and wrap suture around both tendons around the rod
steps.instruction: Pull and wrap suture around both tendons around the rod.

steps.title: Fill the phrenic cavity with 0
steps.instruction: Fill the phrenic cavity with 0.9% saline and suck the distal end of the proximal nerve stump into the suction electrode. The proximal end of the nerve sheath that was retracted in the prior step will form a tight seal with the tip of the suction electrode, preventing any leakage or loss of suction. The tip of the nerve should be visible within the tip of the electrode and both the inner wire and the desheathed portion of the nerve should be fully submerged in saline within the electrode

steps.title: Carefully place the uninsulated tip of...
steps.instruction: Carefully place the uninsulated tip of the outer wire against the proximal stump of the nerve proximal to the tip of the electrode and check recording for signal quality

steps.title: Following completion of surgical procedure, administer...
steps.instruction: Following completion of surgical procedure, administer fluids as follows: 4:1 ratio of Lactated Ringers Solution + Sodium Bicarbonate at 1.0 ml/hr

steps.title: Administer pancuronium bromide (paralytic)
steps.instruction: Administer pancuronium bromide (paralytic): 1g in 1cc delivered via tail vein over a 3 minute period

steps.title: Set baseline CO 2 parameters
steps.instruction: Set baseline CO 2 parameters : Conducted no sooner than 20 minutes after pancuronium bromide delivery

steps.title: Draw an initial blood sample to...
steps.instruction: Draw an initial blood sample to ensure base excess of +/- 3

steps.title: Hyperinflate the lungs over 2 respiratory...
steps.instruction: Hyperinflate the lungs over 2 respiratory cycles by briefly occluding the expiratory line

steps.title: Wait at least 20 minutes before...
steps.instruction: Wait at least 20 minutes before starting the experimental protocol - must have “stable” baseline activity for at least 15 minutes, defined as no trend increases/decreases in nerve activity greater than 5%

steps.title: Take a blood sample during baseline...
steps.instruction: Take a blood sample during baseline separated by at least 5 minutes to establish baseline blood gas values: Criteria = PaO2 above 150 mmHg; SBE within +/- 3. Wait at least 5-minutes after blood sample before starting experimental protocol

steps.title: Set inspiratory-triggered stimulation threshold
steps.instruction: Set inspiratory-triggered stimulation threshold: using contralateral integrated phrenic amplitude, set horizontal cursor in the middle of the phrenic amplitude.

steps.title: Attach left phrenic electrode to stimulator
steps.instruction: Attach left phrenic electrode to stimulator. Right phrenic electrode is recorded.

steps.title: Deliver first current
steps.instruction: Deliver first current. All stimulation is biphasic, inspiratory triggered stimulation using a wide pulse (each phase 1.0ms) or narrow pulse (each phase 0.1ms). Stimulation is delivered for 20seconds at pre-determined current. Stimulation is turned off and output is recorded for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Repeat until all 12 currents have been delivered
steps.instruction: Repeat until all 12 currents have been delivered. All currents are randomly presented to each animal so that each current is delivered first in one animal.

steps.title: Draw blood a sample after 4...
steps.instruction: Draw blood a sample after 4 currents and adjust inspired gas concentrations to keep PaCO2 within 2 mmHg of baseline and PaO 2 must remain above 150 mmHg at all blood draws.

steps.title: PaO 2 must remain above 150...
steps.instruction: PaO 2 must remain above 150 mmHg at all subsequent blood draws

steps.title: At the end of the experiment,...
steps.instruction: At the end of the experiment, deliver hypercapnia 12% inspired CO2 for max response.

steps.title: Analysis
steps.instruction: Analysis: Bilateral phrenic activity (amplitude and frequency), blood pressure (systolic, diastolic and mean arterial pressures) and heart rate are analyzed at baseline, and during and after each current and during hypercapnia.
